currentPath.summary: Discovery-first product and business build: convert Trista's clearer founder direction into outward-facing site, messaging, and launch architecture while keeping the dashboard and reporting surfaces current.
focus: Turn Trista's direct answers into durable planning docs.
Translate planning into public-facing messaging and site structure.
Keep the live site and project dashboard current enough to act as a real command surface.
Use heartbeat/autonomous review to keep choosing the next bounded step.

nextSteps.title: Draft outward-facing website/content architecture
nextSteps.why: Planning Pack v1 now exists, so the next useful move is homepage messaging, page structure, and launch-flow definition.

nextSteps.title: Finish dashboard refinement
nextSteps.why: The light passphrase gate and richer project-log treatment need to finish propagating publicly.

nextSteps.title: Continue direct email discovery only for narrower follow-up questions
nextSteps.why: Broad discovery has improved a lot; remaining questions are now tactical and sequencing-oriented.

nextSteps.title: Recommend the first concrete launch asset
nextSteps.why: The next product decision is likely workbook vs. unit study vs. podcast/blog-first sequencing.

completed: Created and published the first live GitHub Pages site at https://origin4124.github.io/.
Replaced the original proof page with a more real Thriving Dancer landing page.
Added a studio scheduling / management concept section to the public site.
Verified the GitHub Pages update after propagation and closed the publish loop.
Sent Telegram notification with the live URL once the site was ready.
Established direct email communication with Trista.

Captured a strong first signal set from Trista: credentials, performance history, teaching background, founder role, age 3–14 mission focus, growth mindset, developmentally appropriate training, and whole-person philosophy.

Captured clearer founder answers: first audience = pre-professional dancers ages 10–16, first launch = podcast + blog, early products = workbook + ballet unit study, and the studio software lane is both internal and potentially product-facing later.
Set up a 2-hour autonomous Thriving Dancer assessment heartbeat to decide and take the next bounded project step.
Built and published a live Thriving Dancer project dashboard at /dashboard.html.
Created a working discovery brief and explicit next-step file inside the project folder.
Created Planning Pack v1 with positioning, audience, offer ladder, launch channels, website structure, and strategic sequence.

blockers.label: Dashboard refinement in progress
blockers.detail: Light passphrase gating and the richer project-log view were added locally and pushed, but public propagation may still lag briefly.

blockers.label: Outlook reliability
blockers.detail: Outlook Web has been flaky under network drops, and replies can fall into draft state instead of sending unless verified carefully.

blockers.label: Native channel runtime
blockers.detail: Native Telegram/Discord channel registration in OpenClaw is still broken, so alerts and messaging depend on workarounds.

telegram.event: Thriving Dancer site became review-ready and complete.
Dashboard passphrase/project-log update finished locally and Outlook was cleaned down to one main tab.

notifications.reviewReadyPolicy: Ping Kirill on Telegram when review is ready and again when the full loop is complete for important operations.
notifications.taskCompletionPreference: Ping Kirill on Telegram every time a task is finished.

items: Set Thriving Dancer as a staged product direction and agreed on discovery first, website first, app later.
Established preference for PM/dev/QA style workflow once implementation ramps up.
Defined a stronger mission around building the business before the studio.
Reviewed Trista's LinkedIn and recognized a broader moat than just dance classes.
Built and published the first public Thriving Dancer landing page on GitHub Pages.
Started direct email communication with Trista and sent follow-up discovery questions.
Added autonomous 2-hour project heartbeat for Thriving Dancer.
Built and published the project dashboard.
Created a working discovery brief and next-step file from Trista's current signal.
Captured stronger founder answers on audience, launch channel, early products, urgency, and long-term software direction.
Started dashboard hardening with a light passphrase gate and richer project-log view.
Created Planning Pack v1 from the founder signal already gathered.

projectLog.title: Set Thriving Dancer as a staged project
projectLog.detail: Agreed on discovery first, website first, and app/product ideas later.

projectLog.title: Clarified project mission
projectLog.detail: Reframed the work as building the business infrastructure, brand, offers, and systems before a physical studio exists.

projectLog.title: Published first public site
projectLog.detail: Created and deployed the first Thriving Dancer landing page on GitHub Pages.

projectLog.title: Started direct founder communication
projectLog.detail: Began email exchange with Trista and gathered initial bio, mission, and philosophy signal.

projectLog.title: Captured stronger founder answers
projectLog.detail: Confirmed first audience, first launch channel, early products, urgency, and long-term software direction.

projectLog.title: Dashboard and heartbeat became operating surfaces
projectLog.detail: The dashboard went live and the 2-hour autonomous project heartbeat started driving next-step assessment.

projectLog.title: Dashboard hardening and cleanup
projectLog.detail: Added a light passphrase screen, richer project-log structure, task-finish notification note, and reduced Outlook to one main useful tab.

projectLog.title: Created Planning Pack v1
projectLog.detail: Turned the founder signal into positioning, audience, offer ladder, first launch channels, website structure, and strategic sequence.
